worked: Pushing back early on the per-folder README proliferation saved 25+ noise files; the single `FOLDERS.md` + generator script + 7 targeted READMEs cover the same ground.

Building checkpoint helpers as scripts first, then wiring SKILL.md to call them, kept the skill prompt readable and made the logic testable in isolation (verified all four resolve modes end-to-end without needing `/catchup` or `/core-dump`).
Marker-based split in `FOLDERS.md` (`<!-- AUTO-GENERATED BELOW -->`) lets policy stay hand-written while census stays fresh — same pattern as `mistake-patterns.md` being derived from `events.jsonl`.
didnt_work: Initial mcp-catalog audit was wrong — used `.servers` JSON key when the actual key is `.catalog`. Self-corrected after rereading the file. Lesson: `jq | keys[]` errors with "null has no keys" can mean the field doesn't exist, not that the JSON is broken.
Bash-tool sandbox restricts `find /tmp` traversal — startup-script dry-run from this conversation reports 0 files; the script works correctly at LaunchAgent execution time outside the sandbox.
The tab-title fix is diagnostic, not curative — won't know if `/dev/tty` or `TTY_PATH` works until logs accumulate over real session activity.
First attempt at `/core-dump` argument-hint update partially failed (one Edit succeeded, the parallel one didn't); had to re-apply. Always re-read after parallel Edits on the same file.
notes: `_last-checkpoint.json` is now LEGACY — still written for back-compat but `resolve.sh` reads the new index first. Migration 0008 (~2 weeks out) removes the legacy write.
Connector MCPs (anything `claude.ai *` prefixed) cannot be removed via `claude mcp remove`; they're managed by the claude.ai web account.
`~/.claude/file-history/`, `projects/`, `tasks/`, `session-env/` and similar UUID-keyed dirs are Anthropic-managed — never modify them. `FOLDERS.md` documents this taxonomy.
The user's environment: single-user macOS, restarts ~weekly (no logout). LaunchAgent `RunAtLoad=true` is effectively "per reboot" for startup-script purposes.
feedback: Strong preference for derived/regenerable indices over hand-maintained ones.
"Durable system > one-shotting or pleasing me" — explicit invitation to push back on questionable scope.
Per-folder discoverability should be proactive (not just doc-link), but agent overhead matters — hinter-style mechanisms preferred.
Skill grouping: filesystem layout doesn't reduce skill-list surface; scoping needs project-level enable list.

pending: Disable `claude.ai Sentry` + `claude.ai Gmail` connectors via claude.ai web → Settings → Connectors (no CLI path for connector-managed MCPs).
Verify tab-title emit log: `tail ~/.claude/logs/tab-title-emit.log` after a few turns — confirms whether `/dev/tty` writes succeed or only `TTY_PATH` does.
`std::claude::startup` will run on next reboot (LaunchAgent loaded this session).
Transcript pruning is conservative: gzip at 6mo, delete at 12mo. No action needed for months.
`prop-20260515-141140-44` — Checkpoint v2 migration cutover (legacy file removal in ~2 weeks)
